title: scaevola frutescens var. sericea
intro: Scaevola frutescens var. sericea, also known as the hairy fan flower, is a flowering plant native to Australia and the Pacific Islands. Traditionally, the leaves of the Scaevola frutescens var. sericea plant have been used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, sore throats, and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the leaves can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness their medicinal properties and provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.
